calendar.matters:
PETITION Petition For Order Authorizing Particular Transaction Transferring Assets To Petitioner And For Increase Of Petitioner'S Minimum Monthly Maintenance
Off Calendar May-21-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trusha Friedeberg and Will Doyle for Petitioner.

The Court stated that it looks like the property was taken out of the trust and distributed individually to the Trustee. Ms. Friedeberg agreed. The Court granted the Petition for Order Authorizing Particular Transaction Transferring Assets to Petitioner, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION Petition For Order Authorizing Particular Transaction Transferring Assets To Petitioner And For Increase Of Petitioner'S Minimum Monthly Maintenance
Off Calendar May-01-2019 Continued To May-15-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION Petition For Order Authorizing Particular Transaction Transferring Assets To Petitioner And For Increase Of Petitioner'S Minimum Monthly Maintenance
Off Calendar Apr-03-2019 Continued To May-01-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg and Will Doyle for Petitioner.

The Court noted that this is a request for the Petitioner's income to be increased, asks where the money comes from and raised the issue of taking property out of the trust. Ms. Friedeberg stated that there are two separate requests, that the income is half of Ms. Chang's from social security and half from the property, and that it would be that Mr. Chang's income be redirected to Ms. Chang. Ms. Friedeberg also requested a continuance to amend her petition to reflect that the property has been taken out of the Trust and stated that she does not think a Guardian Ad Litem is necessary, that there are no minors involved, and that it is not required in the probate code or the local rules. The Petition for Order Authorizing Particular Transaction Transferring Assets to Petitioner, etc. is continued to May 1, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
